description_long: This wooden chapel features a traditional Eastern Orthodox architectural style, characterized by its steeply pitched, brown gabled roofs and a prominent onion dome topped with a cross. The structure is primarily constructed from horizontal wooden planks, with a stone foundation visible at the base. The front entrance is sheltered by a deep porch with wooden steps and a gabled roof, while the sides of the building are relatively plain, punctuated by small windows and a covered walkway. The onion dome sits atop the central roof ridge, adding vertical emphasis and religious symbolism. The overall design is compact, with a rectangular footprint and symmetrical facades. The exterior is finished in natural wood tones, complemented by the darker brown of the roof and the subtle gray of the stone base.
description: A compact wooden chapel with a steep brown gabled roof, onion dome, and cross, set on a stone foundation. The design is traditional and symmetrical, with a sheltered front porch and natural wood finishes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a gabled porch, wooden steps, and the onion dome with cross above.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, rectangular body, stone foundation, and the central onion dome on the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back gable, stone base, and the symmetrical roof structure without an entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the side elevation, stone base, and roof details.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: The chapel features a central onion dome with a cross, which serves as a steeple but does not appear to have a bell tower.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is rectangular, made of wood, and set beneath a gabled porch with wooden steps. It is plain in design, matching the natural wood finish of the chapel, and lacks ornate decorative elements.
